title: cerastium fontanum subsp. vulgare
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. vulgare, also known as common mouse-ear or field mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive rounded leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been utilized to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cerastium fontanum subsp. vulgare include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote the healing of cuts and scrapes, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Cerastium fontanum subsp. vulgare can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
